name: sugar
description: Adds sweetness but contributes to obesity and diabetes when consumed in excess.

name: high fructose corn syrup
description: Highly processed sweetener linked to obesity, fatty liver disease, and metabolic syndrome.

name: palm oil
description: High in saturated fat. Linked to heart disease and environmental concerns.

name: salt
description: Essential in small amounts but excess intake raises blood pressure.

name: sodium
description: Excess sodium intake is linked to high blood pressure and heart disease.

name: monosodium glutamate
description: Flavor enhancer (MSG). May cause headaches or allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.

name: aspartame
description: Artificial sweetener with controversial health research. May trigger headaches.

name: sucralose
description: Artificial sweetener. Generally considered safe but may affect gut bacteria.

name: sodium benzoate
description: Preservative that can form benzene (a carcinogen) when combined with vitamin C.

name: potassium sorbate
description: Preservative generally recognized as safe but may cause skin allergies.

name: tartrazine
description: Yellow food dye (E102). Linked to hyperactivity in children and allergic reactions.

name: red 40
description: Synthetic red dye linked to behavioral issues in children.

name: yellow 5
description: Artificial color linked to hyperactivity and allergic reactions.

name: yellow 6
description: Artificial color linked to allergic reactions and tumors in animal studies.

name: bht
description: Butylated hydroxytoluene (BHT). Synthetic antioxidant preservative; possible carcinogen.

name: bha
description: Butylated hydroxyanisole (BHA). Preservative classified as possible carcinogen.

name: tbhq
description: Tertiary butylhydroquinone. Preservative that may weaken immune response.

name: carrageenan
description: Thickener derived from seaweed. May cause digestive inflammation.

name: xanthan gum
description: Common thickener. Generally considered safe in food amounts.

name: guar gum
description: Natural thickener from guar beans. Generally safe.

name: citric acid
description: Naturally found in citrus fruits. Used as flavor enhancer and preservative.

name: ascorbic acid
description: Vitamin C. Used as a preservative and nutrient.

name: vitamin c
description: Essential vitamin with antioxidant properties.

name: vitamin d
description: Essential vitamin for bone health and immunity.

name: iron
description: Essential mineral for blood health.

name: calcium
description: Essential mineral for strong bones and teeth.

name: wheat flour
description: Basic flour ingredient. Contains gluten.

name: whole wheat
description: Whole grain flour rich in fiber and nutrients.

name: oats
description: Whole grain rich in fiber. Supports heart health.

name: milk
description: Dairy ingredient. Good source of calcium and protein.

name: cocoa
description: Rich in antioxidants. Used in chocolate products.

name: cocoa butter
description: Natural fat from cocoa beans. Used in chocolate making.

name: soy lecithin
description: Emulsifier derived from soybeans. Generally safe.

name: lecithin
description: Natural emulsifier. Helps blend ingredients.

name: vanillin
description: Artificial vanilla flavoring. Safe in small amounts but synthetic.

name: natural flavors
description: Flavoring derived from natural sources. Generally considered safe.

name: artificial flavors
description: Synthetic flavoring compounds. May contain undisclosed chemicals.

name: modified starch
description: Chemically altered starch. Used as a thickener.

name: corn starch
description: Common thickener derived from corn.

name: maltodextrin
description: Highly processed carbohydrate with high glycemic index.

name: dextrose
description: Simple sugar derived from corn. Spikes blood sugar.

name: fructose
description: Fruit sugar. Safe naturally but excess processed fructose is harmful.

name: glucose syrup
description: Liquid sugar with high glycemic index.

name: hydrogenated oil
description: Contains trans fats linked to heart disease.

name: partially hydrogenated oil
description: Major source of artificial trans fats. Very harmful.

name: olive oil
description: Healthy monounsaturated fat. Anti-inflammatory properties.

name: sunflower oil
description: Common cooking oil. Moderate in omega-6 fatty acids.

name: soybean oil
description: Common vegetable oil. High in polyunsaturated fats.

name: sodium nitrite
description: Preservative in processed meats. Linked to cancer risk.

name: sodium nitrate
description: Preservative linked to increased cancer risk in processed meats.

name: propylene glycol
description: Used as a moisture-retaining agent. Also found in antifreeze.

name: polysorbate 80
description: Emulsifier that may cause gut inflammation.

name: acesulfame potassium
description: Artificial sweetener. Limited long-term safety data.

name: stevia
description: Natural zero-calorie sweetener from the stevia plant.

name: honey
description: Natural sweetener with antioxidant properties.

name: water
description: Essential ingredient. No health concerns.

name: protein
description: Essential macronutrient for growth and repair.

name: fiber
description: Dietary fiber supports digestive health.

name: trans fat
description: Artificial fat strongly linked to heart disease.

name: sodium phosphate
description: Additive used to retain moisture. May harm kidney function.

name: caramel color
description: Food coloring that may contain 4-MEI, a possible carcinogen.

name: titanium dioxide
description: White food coloring banned in the EU. Possible DNA damage.

name: canola oil
description: Refined vegetable oil high in omega-6; heavily processed.

name: cottonseed oil
description: High omega-6 oil; often contains pesticide residues.

name: vegetable oil
description: Generic term for refined seed oils; high in omega-6.

name: coconut oil
description: Contains medium-chain triglycerides; moderately healthy in small amounts.

name: butter
description: Natural dairy fat; fine in moderation.

name: lard
description: Animal fat high in saturated fat.

name: margarine
description: Often contains trans fats and artificial additives.

name: interesterified fat
description: Chemically modified fat linked to blood sugar spikes.

name: brown sugar
description: Minimally different from white sugar; still contributes to blood sugar spikes.

name: corn syrup
description: Concentrated sugar syrup that spikes blood glucose quickly.

name: invert sugar
description: Equal mixture of glucose and fructose; highly glycemic.

name: saccharin
description: Oldest artificial sweetener; linked to bladder concerns in animal studies.
longTermEffects: Cancer risk in very high doses (animal studies)

name: sorbitol
description: Sugar alcohol that can cause digestive distress in large amounts.

name: xylitol
description: Natural sugar alcohol with low glycaemic index; good for teeth.
longTermEffects: Digestive discomfort at very high intake

name: erythritol
description: Low-calorie sugar alcohol; well tolerated at moderate amounts.
longTermEffects: Generally safe; very large doses may raise cardiovascular markers per recent studies

name: agave syrup
description: Very high in fructose — higher than table sugar.

name: maple syrup
description: Natural sweetener but still high in sugar.

name: coconut sugar
description: Lower GI than table sugar but still mostly sucrose.

name: molasses
description: By-product of sugar refining; contains some minerals but still high sugar.

name: sodium metabisulphite
description: Sulphite preservative; can trigger asthma reactions.

name: sulphur dioxide
description: Common preservative in dried fruits and wine; asthma trigger.

name: calcium propionate
description: Mould inhibitor in bread; some studies link to behavioural issues in children.

name: nisin
description: Natural peptide preservative derived from bacteria; generally safe.

name: sorbic acid
description: Naturally occurring acid used as a mould inhibitor; well tolerated.

name: acetic acid
description: Vinegar — safe and natural preservative.

name: lactic acid
description: Natural preservative from fermentation; safe.

name: mono and diglycerides
description: Emulsifiers that may contain hidden trans fats.

name: sodium stearoyl lactylate
description: Emulsifier from stearic acid; generally safe but highly processed.
longTermEffects: Gut microbiome changes at high intake

name: carboxymethyl cellulose
description: Synthetic emulsifier linked to gut inflammation and microbiome disruption in studies.

name: polysorbate 60
description: Synthetic emulsifier; related to polysorbate 80 with similar concerns.

name: agar
description: Natural seaweed-derived gelling agent; safe.

name: pectin
description: Natural fruit-derived gelling agent; safe and beneficial fibre.

name: locust bean gum
description: Natural thickener from carob seeds; safe.

name: blue 1
description: Synthetic dye (Brilliant Blue); linked to hyperactivity and possible carcinogenic concerns.
longTermEffects: Possible DNA damage at high doses

name: blue 2
description: Synthetic dye (Indigo Carmine); banned in some countries.

name: red 3
description: Erythrosine; thyroid tumour risk in animal studies; banned in cosmetics in the US.

name: green 3
description: Fast Green FCF; limited safety data.

name: caramel colour
description: Class IV caramel contains 4-MEI, a possible carcinogen.

name: annatto
description: Natural colouring from seeds; can trigger hyperactivity in sensitive children.

name: beta carotene
description: Natural orange pigment; precursor to Vitamin A.
longTermEffects: Safe; may be harmful as supplement in smokers

name: turmeric
description: Natural yellow spice with anti-inflammatory properties.

name: disodium inosinate
description: Flavour enhancer (IMP); usually combined with MSG.

name: disodium guanylate
description: Flavour enhancer (GMP); often used with MSG.

name: yeast extract
description: Contains free glutamates; similar effect to MSG.

name: autolysed yeast
description: Contains free glutamates similar to MSG.

name: enriched flour
description: Refined white flour with synthetic vitamins added back after processing.

name: bleached flour
description: Chemically whitened flour; bleaching agents may leave residues.
longTermEffects: Alloxan residues may damage pancreatic cells

name: semolina
description: Coarsely ground durum wheat; reasonable nutritional profile.

name: whole grain
description: Intact grain with bran and germ; high fibre and nutrients.

name: rice flour
description: Gluten-free flour alternative; easily digestible.

name: corn flour
description: Gluten-free starch from maize; fine in moderation.

name: whey protein
description: High-quality dairy protein; excellent amino acid profile.

name: casein
description: Slow-digesting dairy protein; good for sustained nutrition.

name: soy protein
description: Plant protein; may affect hormone levels due to phytoestrogens.

name: pea protein
description: Plant-based protein; hypoallergenic and well tolerated.

name: skim milk powder
description: Dehydrated low-fat milk; good source of protein and calcium.

name: cream
description: High-fat dairy; fine in moderation.
longTermEffects: High saturated fat at large quantities

name: cheese
description: Fermented dairy; good source of calcium and protein.

name: egg
description: Excellent source of protein and essential nutrients.

name: vinegar
description: Acetic acid solution; natural and safe.

name: garlic
description: Natural flavouring with antimicrobial and cardiovascular benefits.

name: onion
description: Natural vegetable; rich in antioxidants.

name: tomato
description: Rich in lycopene and Vitamin C.

name: pepper
description: Common spice; contains piperine which aids nutrient absorption.

name: cinnamon
description: Spice with blood sugar regulating properties.

name: vanilla extract
description: Natural flavouring from vanilla beans.

name: niacin
description: Vitamin B3; essential nutrient added to enriched foods.

name: riboflavin
description: Vitamin B2; essential for energy production.

name: thiamine
description: Vitamin B1; essential for nerve function.

name: folic acid
description: Synthetic form of folate; especially important during pregnancy.

name: potassium chloride
description: Salt substitute; safe in normal amounts.
longTermEffects: High doses can affect heart rhythm

name: magnesium oxide
description: Mineral supplement; generally safe.

name: zinc
description: Essential mineral for immune function.

name: iodine
description: Essential trace mineral for thyroid function.

name: brominated vegetable oil
description: Banned in many countries; accumulates in the body and may cause neurological harm.

name: potassium bromate
description: Flour improver banned in EU, UK, Canada; classified possible carcinogen by IARC.

name: azodicarbonamide
description: Dough conditioner banned in EU and Australia; linked to respiratory issues.

name: silicon dioxide
description: Anti-caking agent; safe in food-grade amounts but nanoparticle concerns exist.

name: sodium aluminium sulphate
description: Leavening agent containing aluminium; accumulation may affect brain health.

name: carmine
description: Red dye from crushed insects (E120); can trigger severe allergies.

name: olestra
description: Fake fat that causes digestive distress and depletes fat-soluble vitamins.

name: refined oil
description: Heavily processed oil stripped of nutrients; high in omega-6.

name: edible vegetable oil
description: Generic term for refined vegetable oils.

name: iodised salt
description: Salt with added iodine; prevents iodine deficiency.
longTermEffects: High sodium intake risks at excess

name: chilli powder
description: Ground dried chillies; contains capsaicin with anti-inflammatory properties.

name: cumin
description: Spice rich in antioxidants; supports digestion.

name: coriander
description: Herb and spice with antioxidant properties.

name: mustard
description: Condiment with anti-inflammatory glucosinolates.

name: besan
description: Chickpea flour; high protein and fibre, low GI.

name: maida
description: Refined white flour common in Indian snacks; very high GI.

name: ajinomoto
description: Brand name for monosodium glutamate (MSG); same concerns as MSG.

name: acidity regulator
description: Generic term for acids/salts that control pH; identity matters.

name: antioxidant
description: Generic additive term; actual safety depends on which antioxidant is used.

name: stabiliser
description: Generic term for texture-improving additives.

name: humectant
description: Moisture-retaining additive; glycerol and propylene glycol are common examples.

name: permitted food colour
description: Vague labelling — actual dye not disclosed; may include harmful azo dyes.

name: nature identical flavouring
description: Synthetic copy of natural flavours; may contain undisclosed compounds.
